topic: A person discovers their teenage diary has been published online
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification hit my phone like a physical blow. I stared at the screen, my jaw tightening until my molars ached. It was a link, a headline flashing across my feed, and beneath it, a snippet of text—my words. My teenage diary. It was out there, plastered across some anonymous blog. A cold, tight knot formed in my stomach, spreading outward like ice water. I felt a strange, hollow emptiness where my usual irritation should have been. I slowly lowered the phone, the glass cool against my palm. I didn't move to delete it; instead, I just leaned back in my chair, the sound of my own breathing suddenly too loud in the quiet room. Every nerve ending felt exposed, raw, and intensely visible.

[story 2]

I found the link tucked away in a forgotten folder on my old laptop. When the article loaded, a wave of hot, prickly anger washed over me. Reading my own rambling confessions, the carefully guarded secrets I thought were safe behind my bedroom door, felt like watching someone pick apart a fragile, private sculpture. I gripped the edge of the desk, knuckles whitening. I let out a short, sharp exhale, a sound more like a hiss than a breath. My eyes scanned the comments section, and a bitter taste rose in my throat. I didn't bother logging out. I just closed the laptop with a decisive snap, the resulting silence in the room feeling heavy and charged with resentment.

[story 3]

It wasn't the initial shock that got to me; it was the sickening realization of how easily everything could be weaponized. I scrolled through the published piece, and a slow, deliberate tightening began around my chest. My shoulders hunched up toward my ears, a defensive posture I hadn't realized I was adopting. I let out a low, almost inaudible sound of disbelief, a tight, humorless little noise. I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of my small room. Each step was measured, heavy with indignation. I ran a hand through my hair, pulling it roughly, trying to physically shake off the feeling of utter violation.
